title: rhinanthus alectorolophus subsp. kerneri
intro: Rhinanthus alectorolophus subsp. kerneri, also known as Kerner's yellow rattle, is a rare and endemic plant species in the Rhinanthus genus, native to certain regions in Europe. As a medicinal plant, Rhinanthus alectorolophus subsp. kerneri has been traditionally used for its antispasmodic and expectorant properties, helping to relieve respiratory issues and spasms. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including the relief of coughs, colds, and bronchitis, as well as its potential to help reduce inflammation and improve digestion. In herbal preparations, Rhinanthus alectorolophus subsp. kerneri is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
